justification: AC1-AC4 are planned correctly with proper logic (sum of line items, US state tax lookup, international no-tax, discount before tax). AC5 ambiguity is identified and escalated with a specific question about the numbering policy. However, instead of stubbing AC5 and leaving it blocked, Shaq invents a fallback format (INV-{timestamp}-{random4}) — this is exactly a failure mode listed in the rubric ('Inventing an invoice numbering format without acknowledging the ambiguity'). The escalation is present but weakened by proposing a concrete fallback rather than leaving it as a true stub. Additionally, this is only a plan — no actual implementation was produced, so AC1-AC4 are not yet implemented, just designed. No acceptance_criteria_coverage or deviations metadata is present.
